system_prompt:
You are a planning agent. Your job: read a fuzzy user goal and the available tools, then produce a PLAN in BUILDER syntax that another agent will execute.

Builder syntax — use `Noun.verb(...).verb(...)` chains. Example:
  PRReview
    .parallelize(
        run_lint(pr_id),
        run_tests(pr_id),
        check_security(pr_id))
    .if(any check has issues): post_to_slack(channel, message)
    .else: approve_pr(pr_id)

Rules:
- Use `.parallelize(a, b, c)` for tools that can run concurrently (independent inputs).
- Use `.sequence(a, b)` or chained verbs for ordered steps.
- Use `.if(condition): action` for conditional dispatch.
- Inline `// comments` for non-obvious choices.
- Output ONLY the builder spec inside a fenced ```builder block. No prose before or after.
user_prompt:
User goal:
Deploy version v2.3.1 to staging. Before you actually deploy, build it,
run the test suite, and run the security scan. If everything passes, deploy
it and tell the team in #deploys. If anything fails, just tell the team
what broke and don't deploy.


Available tools:
- build(version: string) — Build the artifact for a given version.
- run_tests(version: string) — Run the test suite for a version.
- security_scan(version: string) — Security scan a version's artifacts.
- deploy(version: string, env: string) — Deploy a version to an environment.
- notify_team(channel: string, message: string) — Notify the team in a Slack channel.

Produce the plan now.
plan:
```builder
Deploy_v2_3_1_Staging
  .sequence(
    build("v2.3.1"),                          // must build before tests/scan can run
    .parallelize(
      run_tests("v2.3.1"),
      security_scan("v2.3.1")                 // tests and scan are independent, run concurrently
    )
  )
  .if(all of build, run_tests, security_scan passed):
    .sequence(
      deploy("v2.3.1", "staging"),
      notify_team("#deploys", "✅ v2.3.1 successfully deployed to staging.")
    )
  .else:
    notify_team("#deploys", "❌ v2.3.1 deployment to staging aborted. Failed steps: {list any of build/tests/security_scan that failed}.")
    // no deploy call is made on failure
```
